name: loop-kit-dev
description: [DEV] Turn-gated loop plugin that keeps one turn alive until explicit user stop and selects the right internal loop mode from turn-gate.

interface.shortDescription: [DEV] Turn-gated continuity with internal execution, refinement, review, and readiness loops driven from one main surface.
interface.longDescription: [DEV] Provides a narrow operational plugin where turn-gate is the main user-facing surface. It keeps one turn alive until the user explicitly ends the work, runs each flow through preparation, work, verification, and reporting, uses deep-interview plus flow-list design for user-message-driven preparation, records user-message intake and planned-flow design as an operational-preparation flow when it owns session plan, flow-list, scope, or approval-boundary artifacts, treats execution planned flows as cohesive reviewable or commit-sized change units rather than phase checklists or only direct user-visible value, keeps pure final QA/readiness/reporting inside verification/reporting or handoff unless it owns a distinct artifact change, collects enough initial information, expected risky actions, and approval boundaries to execute the planned flow list, inspects current state and scope for existing-flow preparation, selects the right internal loop mode for autonomous execution, refinement, review findings, or readiness, and provides turn-gate-self-drive as an overlay for running prepared planned flows through bounded subagent question routing until final commit-readiness reporting handoff.
defaultPrompt: Keep this turn alive until I explicitly stop and reopen the next flow with explicit choices.
Run each turn-gated flow as preparation, work, verification, and reporting.
Treat planned flows as cohesive reviewable or commit-sized change units, not analysis/work/verification phase slices and not only direct user-visible value.
Keep pure final QA, consistency checks, verification-result reporting, and commit-readiness reporting out of the planned flow list unless they own a distinct artifact/change unit.
For user-message preparation, use deep-interview alignment and turn the result into a planned flow list before execution.
Treat user-message intake and planned-flow design as an operational-preparation flow when it owns session plan, flow-list, scope, or approval-boundary artifacts.

Collect enough information during user-message preparation to execute planned flows with clear boundaries, non-goals, verification expectations, expected risky actions, and user-gated approval checkpoints.
For existing-flow preparation, inspect scope, current state, target files, and verification expectations before work.
Use turn-gate as the main controller and choose the right internal loop for the current phase.
Handle this as a turn-gated loop where autonomous execution, refinement, review, and readiness modes are selected inside turn-gate.
Use turn-gate-self-drive when prepared planned flows should continue through bounded subagent decisions without waiting for me.
When self-driving a planned flow sequence, report commit-readiness after the last change-unit flow; do not treat it as a new planned flow or commit execution approval.
Do not expose direct loop entrypoints; drive the work through turn-gate.
